name: mating-in-captivity
description: Esther Perel's Mating in Captivity: Reconciling the Erotic and the Domestic — a couples psychology and erotic intelligence toolkit exploring why love and desire are in tension, how children affect intimacy, the need for separateness, and how to cultivate lasting passion within long-term relationships.
useCase: Understand the paradox of desire in long-term relationships — learn erotic intelligence, create erotic space, and navigate the tension between security and passion.
knownRisks: The book challenges deeply held cultural beliefs about love and monogamy — may feel uncomfortable
Some readers may interpret Perel as justifying affairs (she does not — she analyzes them)
The framework is primarily Western and may not translate to all cultural contexts
